en.summary: Imam Ja'far al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) teaches two interconnected ethical principles in this hadith. First, that neglecting to correct a brother's wrongdoing when one is able constitutes a form of betrayal, reflecting the Islamic duty of mutual accountability (nasiha). Second, that associating with fools inevitably leads to moral corruption, since companionship shapes one's character. This teaching was delivered in the context of the close-knit Muslim community of Kufa and Medina, where the concept of religious brotherhood (ukhuwwa) carried deep obligations.

key_terms.خانه: Betrayed him - khiyanah (betrayal) is a serious moral failing in Islamic ethics, here applied to neglect of communal duty
key_terms.مصادقة: Befriending, forming close friendship - implies deep, intimate companionship rather than casual acquaintance
key_terms.الأحمق: The fool - one lacking wisdom and sound judgment, whose company corrupts character
key_terms.يتخلق بأخلاقه: Adopt his character/morals - the principle that companionship shapes moral disposition

en.seo_question: What did Imam Sadiq say about correcting your brother and avoiding friendship with fools?

ur.summary: امام جعفر صادق علیہ السلام نے فرمایا کہ بھائی کی غلطی پر خاموش رہنا خیانت ہے اور احمق سے دوستی انسان کے اخلاق بگاڑ دیتی ہے۔

key_terms.خانه: خیانت کی - اسلامی اخلاقیات میں فریضے کی ادائیگی سے غفلت

ur.seo_question: امام صادق نے بھائی کی اصلاح اور احمق سے دوستی کے بارے میں کیا فرمایا؟

tr.summary: İmam Ca'fer es-Sâdık (a.s.) iki ahlaki prensip öğretir: kardeşinin hatasını düzeltmemek ihanettir ve ahmakla dostluk insanın ahlakını bozar.

key_terms.خانه: İhanet etti - toplumsal sorumluluğu ihmal etme

tr.seo_question: İmam Sadık kardeşin düzeltilmesi ve ahmakla dostluk hakkında ne buyurmuştur?

fa.summary: امام جعفر صادق علیه السلام دو اصل اخلاقی را بیان می‌فرماید: ترک نهی از منکر برادر خیانت است و دوستی با احمق اخلاق انسان را فاسد می‌کند.

key_terms.الأحمق: احمق - کسی که فاقد خرد و عقل است

fa.seo_question: امام صادق درباره اصلاح برادر و دوستی با احمق چه فرمودند؟

id.summary: Imam Ja'far al-Shadiq (a.s.) mengajarkan dua prinsip etika: mengabaikan kesalahan saudara ketika mampu mengoreksinya adalah pengkhianatan, dan berteman dengan orang bodoh merusak akhlak seseorang.

key_terms.الأحمق: Orang bodoh, orang yang tidak berakal

id.seo_question: Apa yang dikatakan Imam Shadiq tentang mengoreksi saudara dan menghindari berteman dengan orang bodoh?

bn.summary: ইমাম সাদিক (আ.) দুটি নৈতিক নীতি শিক্ষা দিয়েছেন: ভাইয়ের ভুল সংশোধন না করা বিশ্বাসঘাতকতা এবং বোকার সঙ্গ চরিত্র নষ্ট করে।
bn.seo_question: ইমাম সাদিক ভাইয়ের সংশোধন এবং বোকার সঙ্গ এড়ানো সম্পর্কে কী বলেছেন?

es.summary: El Imam Sadiq (P) enseña dos principios éticos: no corregir al hermano cuando se puede es traición, y la amistad con necios corrompe el carácter.

key_terms.خانه: Lo ha traicionado - negligencia en el deber fraternal

es.seo_question: ¿Qué dijo el Imam Sadiq sobre corregir al hermano y evitar la amistad con necios?

fr.summary: L'Imam Sadiq (P) enseigne deux principes éthiques : négliger de corriger son frère quand on le peut est une trahison, et fréquenter les sots corrompt le caractère.

key_terms.خانه: L'a trahi - manquement au devoir fraternel

fr.seo_question: Qu'a dit l'Imam Sadiq sur la correction fraternelle et l'amitié avec les sots ?

de.summary: Imam Sadiq (F) lehrt zwei ethische Grundsätze: Die Unterlassung brüderlicher Ermahnung ist Verrat, und der Umgang mit Narren verdirbt den Charakter.

key_terms.خانه: Hat ihn verraten - Versäumnis der brüderlichen Pflicht

de.seo_question: Was sagte Imam Sadiq über die Ermahnung des Bruders und die Freundschaft mit Narren?

ru.summary: Имам Садик (мир ему) учит двум этическим принципам: пренебрежение исправлением брата есть предательство, а дружба с глупцами портит нравы.
ru.seo_question: Что сказал Имам Садик об исправлении брата и дружбе с глупцами?

zh.summary: 伊玛目萨迪格（愿主赐他平安）教导了两个伦理原则：有能力却不纠正兄弟的过错是背叛，与愚者交友会败坏品德。

key_terms.خانه: 背叛了他——忽视兄弟义务

zh.seo_question: 伊玛目萨迪格关于纠正兄弟和避免与愚者交友说了什么？

isnad_matn.isnad_ar: حَدَّثَنَا الشَّيْخُ الْفَقِيهُ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عَلِيِّ بْنِ الْحُسَيْنِ بْنِ مُوسَى ابْنِ بَابَوَيْهِ الْقُمِّيُّ رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ الْوَلِيدِ رَحِمَهُ اللَّهُ قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا الْحَسَنُ بْنُ مَتِيلٍ الدَّقَّاقُ قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ الْبَرْقِيُّ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَنْ يُونُسَ بْنِ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ عَنْ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ الْحَجَّاجِ قَالَ سَمِعْتُ الصَّادِقَ عَلَيْهِ السَّلَامُ يَقُولُ
isnad_matn.matn_ar: مَنْ رَأَى أَخَاهُ عَلَى أَمْرٍ يَكْرَهُهُ فَلَمْ يَرُدَّهُ عَنْهُ وَهُوَ يَقْدِرُ عَلَيْهِ فَقَدْ خَانَهُ وَمَنْ لَمْ يَجْتَنِبْ مُصَادَقَةَ الْأَحْمَقِ أَوْشَكَ أَنْ يَتَخَلَّقَ بِأَخْلَاقِهِ

narrators.name_ar: الشَّيْخُ الْفَقِيهُ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عَلِيِّ بْنِ الْحُسَيْنِ بْنِ مُوسَى ابْنِ بَابَوَيْهِ الْقُمِّيُّ
narrators.name_en: al-Shaykh al-Faqih Abu Ja'far Muhammad ibn Ali ibn al-Husayn ibn Musa ibn Babawayh al-Qummi
narrators.known_identity: Al-Shaykh al-Saduq (d. 381 AH), author of Man La Yahduruhu al-Faqih and al-Amali

narrators.name_ar: مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ الْوَلِيدِ
narrators.name_en: Muhammad ibn al-Hasan ibn Ahmad ibn al-Walid
narrators.known_identity: Muhammad ibn al-Hasan ibn Ahmad ibn al-Walid al-Qummi (d. 343 AH), prominent Shia hadith scholar and teacher of al-Saduq
al-Hasan ibn Matil al-Daqqaq, Qummi narrator in the chains of al-Saduq

narrators.name_ar: أَحْمَدُ بْنُ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ الْبَرْقِيُّ
narrators.known_identity: Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn Khalid al-Barqi (d. 274 AH), author of al-Mahasin

narrators.ambiguity_note: Refers to Muhammad ibn Khalid al-Barqi, father of Ahmad. Identification based on preceding narrator.

narrators.known_identity: Yunus ibn Abd al-Rahman (d. 208 AH), a prominent companion of Imam al-Kazim (AS) and Imam al-Rida (AS)
Abd al-Rahman ibn al-Hajjaj al-Bajali al-Kufi, a prominent companion of Imam al-Sadiq (AS) and Imam al-Kazim (AS)
Imam Ja'far al-Sadiq (AS), the sixth Imam (d. 148 AH)

similar_content_hints.description: Numerous hadiths in al-Kafi and other collections about enjoining good and forbidding evil (al-amr bil-ma'ruf wal-nahy 'an al-munkar) as an obligation upon every Muslim, with similar emphasis on it being a form of betrayal to neglect.

Hadiths about choosing good companions and avoiding bad company, found across al-Kafi (Book of Social Ethics) and Nahj al-Balagha, teaching that a person is influenced by the character of their associates.
Sayings from Imam Ali (AS) in Nahj al-Balagha about the dangers of befriending the foolish and how companionship shapes character.
